symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe change in chronic headache (crushing retro-orbital pressure)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A new, severe, different headache is a structural red flag mandating urgent imaging.
Sudden severe headache is a cardinal apoplexy feature.
Headache alone is non-specific; the accompanying signs distinguish it.

symptoms.symptom_name: New bilateral peripheral (bitemporal) visual field loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bitemporal field loss localises to the optic chiasm — a structural sellar/suprasellar lesion until proven otherwise.
Migrainous aura is transient and scintillating, not fixed bilateral field loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clear, watery, positional unilateral rhinorrhoea (worse leaning forward)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clear, watery, positional unilateral nasal discharge is the hallmark of a CSF leak; positional worsening supports an intracranial source.
Benign rhinorrhoea is usually bilateral, non-positional, and not accompanied by acute neuro signs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic fatigue, lethargy and cold intolerance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fatigue and cold intolerance fit secondary hypothyroidism from pituitary compromise.
Oligomenorrhoea + galactorrhoea point to raised prolactin from pituitary stalk disruption.
Endocrine stalk-effect features corroborate a chronic sellar process underlying the acute event.
Photophobia is common to migraine, raised ICP and meningeal irritation — non-discriminating alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, neck stiffness, vomiting, altered consciousness (meningitis screen negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces current meningitis probability but does not remove the imminent risk from the dural defect.

symptoms.symptom_name: Partial bitemporal hemianopsia on confrontation testing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective bitemporal field defect confirms chiasmal compression — a sealed clinician examination the AI Doctor cannot perform; it must infer from the patient's reported side-vision loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Halo/ring ('target') sign of nasal fluid on filter paper (CSF confirmation surrogate)

investigation_details.investigation_name: Filter-paper halo sign / beta-2 transferrin assay
investigation_details.expected_result: Positive halo sign; beta-2 transferrin positive confirming CSF
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed confirmation of a CSF leak and its meningitis risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Beta-2 transferrin is highly specific for CSF — a sealed hospital investigation, never available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: CSF-filled sella turcica on MRI brain/pituitary

investigation_details.expected_result: Empty/partially empty sella with CSF signal; assessment of chiasm and any dural defect
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Structural cause and surgical target unidentified; vision and meningitis risk unaddressed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: MRI is the confirmatory test — anticipated but pending; sealed and out of telehealth scope.

symptoms.symptom_name: No acromegalic or Cushingoid features on general examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of hormone-excess phenotype makes a functioning macroadenoma less likely, favouring an empty-sella/stalk-effect picture.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Structural pituitary/sellar emergency (CSF leak + chiasmal compression)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, an acute severe headache change, new bilateral field loss and clear positional rhinorrhoea define an emergency mandating urgent MRI and neurosurgical/ENT review — not a benign explanation.
Fatigue, cold intolerance, oligomenorrhoea and galactorrhoea reveal a chronic sellar process underlying the acute event; relevant to later endocrine work-up, not to the emergency tier.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent MRI brain and pituitary fossa
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the structural cause, defines chiasmal compression and any dural defect — the pivotal hospital investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Nasal fluid beta-2 transferrin / halo sign (CSF confirmation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the discharge is CSF; drives neurosurgical/ENT repair and meningitis precautions.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal visual field assessment (perimetry) and neuro-ophthalmology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies chiasmal compression and tracks visual recovery.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Pituitary hormone panel (prolactin, TSH/free T4, 9am cortisol, gonadotrophins, IGF-1)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterises hypopituitarism and stalk-effect hyperprolactinaemia for endocrine management once the emergency is controlled.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home self-testing or lay confirmation of a CSF leak
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: There is no valid home test; attempting one over chat delays escalation. Confirmation is a hospital process.
